commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: And with him they crucified two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left. Augustine of Hippo Amid the courtroom of the cross, one robber who believed was freed, the other who insulted him was condemned. He was then signifying in advance what he would do concerning the living and the dead, putting some on his right and some on his left. The one robber was like to those who would be on the left; the other, to those who would be on the right. He who was being judged was anticipating final judgment.

commentaries.author: Cyril of Jerusalem
commentaries.content: Of the robbers crucified with him, it was said: “He was reckoned among the wicked.” Up to this time both were wicked, but one of them was wicked no longer. For one was wicked to the end, yielding not to salvation, and, though his hands were fastened, he struck blasphemously with his tongue.
